question: Add setter methods `setTitle(string)`, `setAuthor(string)`, and `setIsbn(string)` to the `Book` class. For `setTitle()`, ensure that the title cannot be set to an empty string; if an empty string is provided, print an error and do not update.

test_cases.expected:
Initial Book State:
  Title: Learning C++
  Author: Bjarne Stroustrup
  ISBN: 0-201-0345-X
--------------------------------
After updating author:
  Title: Learning C++
  Author: B. Stroustrup
  ISBN: 0-201-0345-X
--------------------------------
After updating ISBN:
  Title: Learning C++
  Author: B. Stroustrup
  ISBN: 978-0321992789
--------------------------------
Attempting to set title to an empty string (expecting no change and verbose message)...
Attention: The provided title string was found to be completely empty. Therefore, the book's title will not be modified from its current value. Please provide a non-empty title.
After attempting to set empty title (should be unchanged):
  Title: Learning C++
  Author: B. Stroustrup
  ISBN: 978-0321992789
--------------------------------
After setting a new valid title:
  Title: Effective Modern C++
  Author: B. Stroustrup
  ISBN: 978-0321992789
--------------------------------

errors: Setter methods pass `std::string` arguments by value instead of `const std::string&`, leading to unnecessary string copies and reduced efficiency.
The `setTitle` method uses `newTitleValue.length() == 0` instead of the more idiomatic and potentially clearer `newTitleValue.empty()` for checking empty strings.
The error message for an an empty title is overly verbose, impacting readability.
